maxwell.headline: Ghislaine Maxwell refused to testify before House Oversight Committee unless granted clemency by President Trump, invoking Fifth Amendment in February 2026 hearing.[1][2]
maxwell.summary: In February 2026, Ghislaine Maxwell invoked her Fifth Amendment right during a virtual deposition before the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee, stating she would testify fully only if granted clemency by President Trump, as announced by her attorney David Oscar Markus.[1][2] Lawmakers from both parties rejected the conditional offer, with Chairman James Comer denying her immunity request and stating she committed serious crimes.[2] Her habeas corpus petition was denied on January 13, 2026, exhausting major legal challenges after the Supreme Court declined her appeal.[1] Senator Jacky Rosen announced a resolution opposing any clemency for Maxwell.[4] No clemency petition has been publicly filed, and she remains incarcerated at FPC Bryan with a release date around 2037.[1]

gulf_swf.headline: Saudi PIF soft-launched its 2026-2030 strategy on March 9, 2026, shifting focus to industry, minerals, AI, and tourism while scaling back mega projects.
gulf_swf.summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) soft-launched its new five-year strategy (2026-2030) with key investors at a Riyadh conference on Monday, marking a major recalibration of Vision 2030 amid fiscal pressures and project delays like The Line.[1][2][4] PIF Governor Yasir Al-Rumayyan announced a shift from launching opportunities to accelerating growth through private sector partnerships, offering over 190 investment opportunities worth SAR 40 billion and achieving SAR 591 billion in local content spending from 2020-2024.[2] The strategy emphasizes attracting global capital for sectors including AI, with PIF assets growing to over $1.15 trillion by December 2025.[5] No new developments reported on Mubadala, MGX, ADIA, QIA, GlobalFoundries, or Affinity Partners in recent coverage.

oracle_ellison.headline: Oracle announced $45-50B equity and debt financing plan for 2026 to fund cloud infrastructure expansion amid debt scrutiny and bondholder lawsuit.
oracle_ellison.summary: Oracle revealed plans on February 1, 2026, to raise $45-50 billion in 2026 through a mix of equity (including a $20B at-the-market program) and debt (a one-time senior unsecured bond) to build Oracle Cloud Infrastructure capacity for customers like OpenAI, AMD, Meta, NVIDIA, TikTok, and xAI[1][4]. The announcement drew retail investor attention and faced scrutiny over debt-fueled AI buildout, with Oracle's stock under pressure from high leverage concerns[2]. A proposed class action lawsuit by bondholders alleges Oracle failed to disclose debt needs tied to its OpenAI deal when issuing $18B in notes in September 2025, leading to investor losses after a subsequent $38B loan[2]. No new developments on Stargate contraction, Ellison WBD acquisition, or Paramount-Skydance mergers reported.

epstein_files.headline: DOJ released previously withheld FBI documents from Epstein files alleging sexual assault by Trump on March 10, 2026.
epstein_files.summary: The Justice Department released three FBI interview summaries (302 forms) relating to Donald Trump that were previously withheld, describing allegations of sexual assault on a girl aged 13-15 in the 1980s after introduction by Jeffrey Epstein[1]. Officials claimed the documents were mistakenly coded as duplicates, but only those naming Trump were excluded, violating the Epstein Files Transparency Act[1]. Over 1,000 additional pages were published, with more than a dozen related to Trump abuse allegations following an NPR investigation[2]. The DOJ Epstein Library was last updated March 7, 2026, with ongoing efforts to review and redact per Congressional deadline[3].
